# AI Daily Briefing Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Add a viewer-scoped "AI daily briefing" card to the Home dashboard, generated by an open-source LLM (Llama 3.3 70B on Groq's free tier) through a new Supabase Edge Function.

**Architecture:** A new Edge Function `ai-assistant` dispatches on an `action` field (Phase 1 implements only `action: "briefing"`). It authorizes the caller, fetches the viewer's tasks **under the caller's own JWT** (so RLS scopes everything), builds a compact text context from a pure shared module, calls Groq's OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint, and shapes the reply into `{ text, bullets }` via another pure shared module (falling back to a deterministic count summary if the model output is unusable). The client (`BriefingClient` + `HomeView`) caches one briefing per user per Phoenix day in localStorage and renders it in a card, degrading silently on any failure.

**Tech Stack:** Deno (Supabase Edge Function, TypeScript), plain ESM modules (`.mjs`) for the pure server logic, browser-global vanilla JS for the client (zero-build static SPA), `node --test` for unit tests, Supabase JS v2.

## Global Constraints

- Zero-build static SPA — **no framework, no bundler**. Client JS attaches to the `window.App` global and is loaded via `<script defer>` tags in `app.html`. (See existing `js/views/*.js`.)
- Runtime config: the client Supabase client is `App.supabase`, ready only after `await App.configReady`. Never hardcode URL/key.
- The AI is **read-only** in Phase 1 — it never writes to the database.
- **No new database tables or migrations.** Briefing caching is client-side (localStorage); the server quota guard is a best-effort in-memory counter.
- Edge Function conventions (copy from `supabase/functions/notify-email/index.ts`): strict `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` CORS allowlist that fails closed, `MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES` body cap, a last-resort `try/catch` that returns clean JSON `{ error }` and never a stack trace, and the API key never leaves the server.
- Provider seam: Groq base URL + model id live in **single named constants** (`GROQ_ENDPOINT`, `GROQ_MODEL = "llama-3.3-70b-versatile"`) so a later swap to OpenRouter/Together is a one-line change.
- HQ timezone is `America/Phoenix`; the "today" boundary for the cache key and context comes from `App.utils.todayISO(0)` / `App.utils.toISODate(...)` on the client. No emojis in AI copy.
- Unit tests run via `npm run test:unit` → `node --test "tests/unit/*.test.mjs"`. Pure server modules live under the function dir as `.mjs` and are imported directly; client modules are exercised with the `global.window`/`global.App` stub + `require()` pattern (see `tests/unit/utils-upper.test.mjs`).
- Target Supabase project for deploy is **PROD `qqvmcsvdxhgjooirznrj` (Quest HQ)** — never `rqundirizvojpzhljtdn`.

---

## File Structure

- `supabase/functions/ai-assistant/lib/context.mjs` — **pure.** `buildBriefingContext(tasks, opts)` → compact prompt-ready object. No I/O, no globals.
- `supabase/functions/ai-assistant/lib/shape.mjs` — **pure.** `shapeBriefing(modelText, ctx)` → `{ text, bullets }`; `fallbackBriefing(ctx)` → deterministic non-AI summary. No I/O.
- `supabase/functions/ai-assistant/index.ts` — Deno entrypoint: CORS, auth gate, action dispatch, `briefing` handler (RLS fetch → context → Groq → shape), quota guard.
- `js/services/BriefingClient.js` — browser-global `App.BriefingClient`: cache key/read/write + `fetch(dataStore)` wrapper + client-side response guard.
- `js/services/SupabaseDataStore.js` — add `getBriefing()` invoking the function (modify).
- `js/app.js` — add a `getBriefing` stub to the preview-mode dataStore (modify).
- `js/views/HomeView.js` — render the briefing card, skeleton, refresh control, cache wiring (modify).
- `app.html` — add the `<script defer src="js/services/BriefingClient.js">` tag (modify).
- `css/…` — briefing card styles reuse existing `qhq-card`/tone tokens; a small block appended to the Home stylesheet (modify).
- Tests: `tests/unit/briefing-context.test.mjs`, `tests/unit/briefing-shape.test.mjs`, `tests/unit/briefing-cache.test.mjs`.

---

## Task 1: Pure briefing context builder

**Files:**
- Create: `supabase/functions/ai-assistant/lib/context.mjs`
- Test: `tests/unit/briefing-context.test.mjs`

**Interfaces:**
- Produces: `buildBriefingContext(tasks, opts) -> { today, counts, lines }` where
  - `tasks`: array of `{ id, title, company, due, status, priority, assignee, focusSeq, completedAt, activity }` (the app's task shape).
  - `opts`: `{ me, today, maxItems = 25 }` — `me` = viewer id, `today` = `YYYY-MM-DD`.
  - returns `today` (echoed), `counts` = `{ overdue, dueToday, onHold, completedSince, total }`, and `lines` = an array of short strings (≤ `maxItems`) like `"OVERDUE · Fix the sink · roofing · due 2026-07-10"`. Deterministic order: overdue first, then due-today, then on-hold, then focus order. No task descriptions or comment bodies. Titles truncated to 80 chars.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```javascript
// tests/unit/briefing-context.test.mjs
import { test } from 'node:test';
import assert from 'node:assert/strict';
import { buildBriefingContext } from '../../supabase/functions/ai-assistant/lib/context.mjs';

const T = (o) => ({ id: o.id, title: o.title, company: o.company || 'roofing',
  due: o.due ?? null, status: o.status || 'todo', priority: o.priority || 'medium',
  assignee: o.assignee || 'me', focusSeq: o.focusSeq ?? null,
  completedAt: o.completedAt ?? null, activity: o.activity || [] });
const OPTS = { me: 'me', today: '2026-07-14', maxItems: 25 };

test('classifies and counts overdue / due-today / on-hold', () => {
  const ctx = buildBriefingContext([
    T({ id: 'a', title: 'Late thing', due: '2026-07-10' }),
    T({ id: 'b', title: 'Today thing', due: '2026-07-14' }),
    T({ id: 'c', title: 'Parked thing', status: 'hold' }),
    T({ id: 'd', title: 'Someone elses', due: '2026-07-10', assignee: 'other' }),
  ], OPTS);
  assert.equal(ctx.counts.overdue, 1);
  assert.equal(ctx.counts.dueToday, 1);
  assert.equal(ctx.counts.onHold, 1);
  assert.equal(ctx.counts.total, 3); // 'd' is not the viewer's task
  assert.match(ctx.lines[0], /^OVERDUE · Late thing/);
});

test('counts tasks completed since the given day boundary', () => {
  const ctx = buildBriefingContext([
    T({ id: 'a', title: 'Done today', status: 'done', completedAt: '2026-07-14T09:00:00Z' }),
    T({ id: 'b', title: 'Done last week', status: 'done', completedAt: '2026-07-01T09:00:00Z' }),
  ], OPTS);
  assert.equal(ctx.counts.completedSince, 1);
});

test('respects maxItems and truncates long titles', () => {
  const many = Array.from({ length: 40 }, (_, i) => T({ id: 'x' + i, title: 'x'.repeat(120), due: '2026-07-10' }));
  const ctx = buildBriefingContext(many, { ...OPTS, maxItems: 10 });
  assert.equal(ctx.lines.length, 10);
  assert.ok(ctx.lines[0].includes('x'.repeat(80)) && !ctx.lines[0].includes('x'.repeat(81)));
});

test('empty input yields zero counts and no lines', () => {
  const ctx = buildBriefingContext([], OPTS);
  assert.deepEqual(ctx.counts, { overdue: 0, dueToday: 0, onHold: 0, completedSince: 0, total: 0 });
  assert.deepEqual(ctx.lines, []);
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `node --test tests/unit/briefing-context.test.mjs`
Expected: FAIL — cannot find module `context.mjs` / `buildBriefingContext is not a function`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```javascript
// supabase/functions/ai-assistant/lib/context.mjs
// Pure: turns the viewer's tasks into a compact, prompt-ready context. No I/O,
// no globals, no task descriptions/comment bodies — titles only, truncated.
const DONE = new Set(['done', 'complete', 'completed']);
const trunc = (s, n) => { const t = String(s || ''); return t.length > n ? t.slice(0, n) : t; };

export function buildBriefingContext(tasks, opts) {
  const { me, today, maxItems = 25 } = opts || {};
  const mine = (tasks || []).filter((t) => t && t.assignee === me);

  const isDone = (t) => DONE.has(String(t.status || '').toLowerCase());
  const open = mine.filter((t) => !isDone(t));
  const overdue = open.filter((t) => t.due && t.due < today);
  const dueToday = open.filter((t) => t.due === today);
  const onHold = open.filter((t) => t.status === 'hold');
  const completedSince = mine.filter((t) => {
    if (!isDone(t) || !t.completedAt) return false;
    // completedAt is an ISO instant; compare its date portion to `today`.
    return String(t.completedAt).slice(0, 10) >= today;
  });

  const counts = {
    overdue: overdue.length,
    dueToday: dueToday.length,
    onHold: onHold.length,
    completedSince: completedSince.length,
    total: open.length,
  };

  // Deterministic ordering: overdue, due-today, on-hold, then remaining open by
  // focus order (focusSeq asc, unset last) then soonest due.
  const bySeq = (a, b) => {
    const fa = a.focusSeq == null ? Infinity : a.focusSeq;
    const fb = b.focusSeq == null ? Infinity : b.focusSeq;
    if (fa !== fb) return fa - fb;
    return String(a.due || '9999').localeCompare(String(b.due || '9999'));
  };
  const seen = new Set();
  const push = (arr, out) => { for (const t of arr) { if (!seen.has(t.id)) { seen.add(t.id); out.push(t); } } };
  const ordered = [];
  push(overdue.slice().sort(bySeq), ordered);
  push(dueToday.slice().sort(bySeq), ordered);
  push(onHold.slice().sort(bySeq), ordered);
  push(open.slice().sort(bySeq), ordered);

  const tag = (t) => t.due && t.due < today ? 'OVERDUE'
    : t.due === today ? 'DUE TODAY'
    : t.status === 'hold' ? 'ON HOLD' : 'OPEN';
  const lines = ordered.slice(0, maxItems).map((t) =>
    `${tag(t)} · ${trunc(t.title, 80)} · ${t.company || '—'} · ${t.due ? 'due ' + t.due : 'no due date'}`);

  return { today, counts, lines };
}
```

Expected: PASS (4 tests).

## Task 2: Pure briefing response shaper

**Files:**
- Create: `supabase/functions/ai-assistant/lib/shape.mjs`
- Test: `tests/unit/briefing-shape.test.mjs`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: the `ctx` object from Task 1 (`{ today, counts, lines }`).
- Produces:
  - `shapeBriefing(modelText, ctx) -> { text, bullets, source }` where `text` is a trimmed narrative string, `bullets` is an array of `{ taskId: null, label }` (Phase 1 keeps `taskId` null; the client links by title match later if desired), `source` is `"model"` or `"fallback"`. If `modelText` is empty/whitespace or yields no usable narrative, returns `fallbackBriefing(ctx)`.
  - `fallbackBriefing(ctx) -> { text, bullets, source: "fallback" }` — a deterministic sentence from `ctx.counts`, e.g. `"2 tasks overdue, 1 due today, 1 on hold. 3 completed since yesterday."` and up to 3 bullets drawn from `ctx.lines`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```javascript
// tests/unit/briefing-shape.test.mjs
import { test } from 'node:test';
import assert from 'node:assert/strict';
import { shapeBriefing, fallbackBriefing } from '../../supabase/functions/ai-assistant/lib/shape.mjs';

const CTX = {
  today: '2026-07-14',
  counts: { overdue: 2, dueToday: 1, onHold: 1, completedSince: 3, total: 4 },
  lines: ['OVERDUE · Fix the sink · roofing · due 2026-07-10', 'DUE TODAY · Call client · lumen · due 2026-07-14'],
};

test('shapes a normal model reply into text + bullets', () => {
  const out = shapeBriefing('You have two overdue tasks.\n- Fix the sink first\n- Call the client', CTX);
  assert.equal(out.source, 'model');
  assert.match(out.text, /overdue/i);
  assert.ok(out.bullets.length >= 1 && out.bullets.length <= 3);
});

test('falls back when the model returns empty output', () => {
  const out = shapeBriefing('   ', CTX);
  assert.equal(out.source, 'fallback');
  assert.match(out.text, /overdue/);
});

test('falls back when model output is non-string / malformed', () => {
  assert.equal(shapeBriefing(null, CTX).source, 'fallback');
  assert.equal(shapeBriefing(undefined, CTX).source, 'fallback');
});

test('fallback text is deterministic from counts', () => {
  const a = fallbackBriefing(CTX).text;
  const b = fallbackBriefing(CTX).text;
  assert.equal(a, b);
  assert.match(a, /2 .*overdue/);
});

test('bullets never exceed 3', () => {
  const out = shapeBriefing('Summary line\n- a\n- b\n- c\n- d\n- e', CTX);
  assert.ok(out.bullets.length <= 3);
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `node --test tests/unit/briefing-shape.test.mjs`
Expected: FAIL — module not found / `shapeBriefing is not a function`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```javascript
// supabase/functions/ai-assistant/lib/shape.mjs
// Pure: validates/normalizes the model's reply into { text, bullets }, or a
// deterministic count-based fallback when the model output is unusable.

function pluralize(n, word) { return `${n} ${word}${n === 1 ? '' : 's'}`; }

export function fallbackBriefing(ctx) {
  const c = (ctx && ctx.counts) || { overdue: 0, dueToday: 0, onHold: 0, completedSince: 0 };
  const parts = [];
  if (c.overdue) parts.push(`${pluralize(c.overdue, 'task')} overdue`);
  if (c.dueToday) parts.push(`${c.dueToday} due today`);
  if (c.onHold) parts.push(`${c.onHold} on hold`);
  const head = parts.length ? parts.join(', ') + '.' : 'Nothing overdue or due today.';
  const tail = c.completedSince ? ` ${pluralize(c.completedSince, 'task')} completed since yesterday.` : '';
  const bullets = ((ctx && ctx.lines) || []).slice(0, 3).map((l) => ({ taskId: null, label: l }));
  return { text: (head + tail).trim(), bullets, source: 'fallback' };
}

export function shapeBriefing(modelText, ctx) {
  if (typeof modelText !== 'string' || !modelText.trim()) return fallbackBriefing(ctx);
  const lines = modelText.split('\n').map((l) => l.trim()).filter(Boolean);
  // Bullets = lines starting with a list marker; narrative = the rest.
  const isBullet = (l) => /^([-*•]|\d+[.)])\s+/.test(l);
  const bulletLines = lines.filter(isBullet).map((l) => l.replace(/^([-*•]|\d+[.)])\s+/, ''));
  const narrative = lines.filter((l) => !isBullet(l)).join(' ').trim();
  if (!narrative && !bulletLines.length) return fallbackBriefing(ctx);
  const bullets = bulletLines.slice(0, 3).map((label) => ({ taskId: null, label }));
  return { text: narrative || bulletLines[0], bullets, source: 'model' };
}
```

Expected: PASS (5 tests).

## Task 3: The `ai-assistant` Edge Function

**Files:**
- Create: `supabase/functions/ai-assistant/index.ts`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `buildBriefingContext` (Task 1), `shapeBriefing` + `fallbackBriefing` (Task 2) via relative import.
- Produces: HTTP endpoint. Request `POST { action: "briefing" }`. Success `200 { ok: true, briefing: { text, bullets, source }, generatedAt }`. Errors: `401` missing/invalid JWT, `403` unapproved, `400` unknown action / bad JSON, `429` over daily cap, `502` provider error, `503` not configured.

This is a Deno function — **not** covered by `node --test`. Verified by curl in Task 6.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the function**

```typescript
// supabase/functions/ai-assistant/index.ts
// Supabase Edge Function: ai-assistant
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// The Quest HQ AI assistant backend. Phase 1 implements action "briefing": a
// short, viewer-scoped daily briefing generated by an open-source model
// (Llama 3.3 70B) on Groq's OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
//
// DEPLOY (one-time):
//   1. Create a free account at https://console.groq.com and make an API key.
//   2. Set the function secrets on the PROD project (qqvmcsvdxhgjooirznrj):
//        supabase secrets set GROQ_API_KEY=gsk_xxx
//        supabase secrets set ALLOWED_ORIGINS="https://<your-prod-origin>,http://localhost:5173"
//      (SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY are injected automatically.)
//   3. Deploy:
//        supabase functions deploy ai-assistant
//
// HARDENING:
//   - JWT-verified by default; we additionally require an APPROVED profile.
//   - Task data is fetched under the CALLER'S JWT, so RLS guarantees the model
//     only ever sees tasks the user could already read.
//   - The Groq key never leaves the server. Body size + a best-effort per-user
//     daily cap protect the shared free tier.
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

import { createClient } from "https://esm.sh/@supabase/supabase-js@2";
import { buildBriefingContext } from "./lib/context.mjs";
import { shapeBriefing, fallbackBriefing } from "./lib/shape.mjs";

// Provider seam — swap these two lines to move off Groq.
const GROQ_ENDPOINT = "https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions";
const GROQ_MODEL = "llama-3.3-70b-versatile";

const MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES = 32 * 1024;
const MAX_CONTEXT_ITEMS = 25;
const DAILY_CAP = 10; // best-effort, per user, per UTC day

// Best-effort in-memory counter. Resets on cold start — a soft guard, not a
// hard quota (the client's per-day cache is the primary limiter).
const usage = new Map<string, { day: string; n: number }>();

function corsHeadersFor(req: Request): Record<string, string> {
  const headers: Record<string, string> = {
    "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "authorization, x-client-info, apikey, content-type",
    "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "POST, OPTIONS",
    "Vary": "Origin",
  };
  const allowList = (Deno.env.get("ALLOWED_ORIGINS") ?? "")
    .split(",").map((s) => s.trim()).filter(Boolean);
  if (allowList.length === 0) {
    console.error("[ai-assistant] ALLOWED_ORIGINS is not set — refusing cross-origin responses.");
    return headers;
  }
  const origin = req.headers.get("Origin") ?? "";
  if (allowList.includes(origin)) headers["Access-Control-Allow-Origin"] = origin;
  return headers;
}

function json(req: Request, body: unknown, status = 200): Response {
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(body), {
    status,
    headers: { ...corsHeadersFor(req), "Content-Type": "application/json" },
  });
}

Deno.serve(async (req: Request) => {
  if (req.method === "OPTIONS") return new Response("ok", { headers: corsHeadersFor(req) });
  if (req.method !== "POST") return json(req, { error: "Method not allowed" }, 405);

  try {
    const groqKey = Deno.env.get("GROQ_API_KEY");
    const supabaseUrl = Deno.env.get("SUPABASE_URL");
    const anonKey = Deno.env.get("SUPABASE_ANON_KEY");
    if (!groqKey) return json(req, { error: "AI service is not configured." }, 503);
    if (!supabaseUrl || !anonKey) return json(req, { error: "Service credentials are not available." }, 503);

    const authHeader = req.headers.get("Authorization") ?? "";
    const callerJwt = authHeader.replace(/^Bearer\s+/i, "").trim();
    if (!callerJwt) return json(req, { error: "Missing authorization." }, 401);

    // Client keyed by the ANON apikey but carrying the caller's JWT in the
    // Authorization header: identity + all queries run under the caller's RLS.
    const userClient = createClient(supabaseUrl, anonKey, {
      global: { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${callerJwt}` } },
    });
    const { data: userData, error: userErr } = await userClient.auth.getUser();
    if (userErr || !userData?.user) return json(req, { error: "Not signed in." }, 401);
    const uid = userData.user.id;

    const { data: profile, error: profErr } = await userClient
      .from("profiles").select("approved").eq("id", uid).single();
    if (profErr || !profile || !profile.approved) return json(req, { error: "Not authorized." }, 403);

    const raw = await req.text();
    if (raw.length > MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES) return json(req, { error: "Payload too large." }, 413);
    let payload: { action?: unknown };
    try { payload = JSON.parse(raw || "{}"); } catch { return json(req, { error: "Invalid JSON body." }, 400); }
    if (payload.action !== "briefing") return json(req, { error: "Unknown action." }, 400);

    // Best-effort per-user daily cap.
    const day = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    const u = usage.get(uid);
    const n = u && u.day === day ? u.n : 0;
    if (n >= DAILY_CAP) return json(req, { error: "Daily briefing limit reached. Try again tomorrow." }, 429);
    usage.set(uid, { day, n: n + 1 });

    // ---- fetch the viewer's tasks under RLS -----------------------------
    // Pull a bounded slice of the caller's open + recently-completed tasks.
    const { data: rows, error: taskErr } = await userClient
      .from("tasks")
      .select("id,title,company,due,status,priority,assignee,focus_seq,completed_at")
      .eq("assignee", uid)
      .order("due", { ascending: true })
      .limit(120);
    if (taskErr) {
      console.error("[ai-assistant] task fetch failed", taskErr);
      return json(req, { error: "Could not load your tasks." }, 500);
    }
    const tasks = (rows ?? []).map((r) => ({
      id: r.id, title: r.title, company: r.company, due: r.due, status: r.status,
      priority: r.priority, assignee: r.assignee, focusSeq: r.focus_seq, completedAt: r.completed_at, activity: [],
    }));

    const today = new Intl.DateTimeFormat("en-CA", { timeZone: "America/Phoenix" }).format(new Date());
    const ctx = buildBriefingContext(tasks, { me: uid, today, maxItems: MAX_CONTEXT_ITEMS });

    // ---- call Groq ------------------------------------------------------
    const system = "You are a concise task assistant. Write a 2 to 4 sentence briefing describing what happened and what needs attention today, then up to 3 short bullet lines each naming one specific task. Only reference tasks in the provided context. Plain text, no emojis, no markdown headings.";
    const userMsg = `Today is ${today}.\nCounts: ${JSON.stringify(ctx.counts)}\nTasks:\n${ctx.lines.join("\n") || "(none)"}`;

    let briefing;
    try {
      const res = await fetch(GROQ_ENDPOINT, {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${groqKey}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          model: GROQ_MODEL, temperature: 0.4, max_tokens: 350,
          messages: [{ role: "system", content: system }, { role: "user", content: userMsg }],
        }),
      });
      if (!res.ok) {
        console.error("[ai-assistant] provider rejected", { status: res.status });
        // Degrade to a deterministic summary rather than failing the card.
        briefing = fallbackBriefing(ctx);
      } else {
        const data = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));
        const text = data?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content ?? "";
        briefing = shapeBriefing(text, ctx);
      }
    } catch (e) {
      console.error("[ai-assistant] provider fetch threw", e);
      briefing = fallbackBriefing(ctx);
    }

    return json(req, { ok: true, briefing, generatedAt: new Date().toISOString() });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("[ai-assistant] uncaught", err);
    return json(req, { error: "Internal error." }, 500);
  }
});
```

## Task 4: Client BriefingClient (cache + fetch)

**Files:**
- Create: `js/services/BriefingClient.js`
- Modify: `js/services/SupabaseDataStore.js` (add `getBriefing`)
- Modify: `js/app.js` (preview-mode `getBriefing` stub)
- Modify: `app.html` (script tag)
- Test: `tests/unit/briefing-cache.test.mjs`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `App.utils.todayISO(0)` (Phoenix `YYYY-MM-DD`), a `dataStore.getBriefing()` returning `{ ok, briefing, error? }`.
- Produces `App.BriefingClient` with **static pure** helpers (tested):
  - `cacheKey(userId, dateISO) -> string` → `qhq.briefing.<userId>.<dateISO>`.
  - `readCache(storage, userId, dateISO) -> briefing|null` → parses JSON, returns null on miss/parse error.
  - `writeCache(storage, userId, dateISO, briefing) -> void`.
  - `guard(briefing) -> briefing|null` → returns the object only if it has a non-empty string `text` and an array `bullets`; else null.
  - and an async instance/`fetch(dataStore)` used by the view (not unit-tested; exercised manually).

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```javascript
// tests/unit/briefing-cache.test.mjs
import { test } from 'node:test';
import assert from 'node:assert/strict';
import { createRequire } from 'node:module';
const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);

global.window = global.window || {};
global.App = global.window.App = { utils: { todayISO: () => '2026-07-14' } };
require('../../js/services/BriefingClient.js');
const BC = global.App.BriefingClient;

// Minimal localStorage stand-in.
function memStore() {
  const m = new Map();
  return { getItem: (k) => (m.has(k) ? m.get(k) : null), setItem: (k, v) => m.set(k, String(v)), removeItem: (k) => m.delete(k) };
}

test('cacheKey is namespaced by user + date', () => {
  assert.equal(BC.cacheKey('u1', '2026-07-14'), 'qhq.briefing.u1.2026-07-14');
});

test('write then read round-trips the briefing', () => {
  const s = memStore();
  const b = { text: 'hi', bullets: [], source: 'model' };
  BC.writeCache(s, 'u1', '2026-07-14', b);
  assert.deepEqual(BC.readCache(s, 'u1', '2026-07-14'), b);
});

test('read returns null on miss and on malformed JSON', () => {
  const s = memStore();
  assert.equal(BC.readCache(s, 'u1', '2026-07-14'), null);
  s.setItem('qhq.briefing.u1.2026-07-14', '{not json');
  assert.equal(BC.readCache(s, 'u1', '2026-07-14'), null);
});

test('guard rejects briefings without usable text/bullets', () => {
  assert.equal(BC.guard(null), null);
  assert.equal(BC.guard({ text: '', bullets: [] }), null);
  assert.equal(BC.guard({ text: 'ok', bullets: 'nope' }), null);
  const good = { text: 'ok', bullets: [] };
  assert.equal(BC.guard(good), good);
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `node --test tests/unit/briefing-cache.test.mjs`
Expected: FAIL — `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'cacheKey')`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```javascript
// js/services/BriefingClient.js
// Client wrapper around the ai-assistant edge function's "briefing" action:
// per-user, per-Phoenix-day localStorage cache + a defensive response guard.
// Pure helpers are static so they can be unit-tested under node.
window.App = window.App || {};

App.BriefingClient = class BriefingClient {
  constructor({ dataStore }) { this.dataStore = dataStore; }

  static cacheKey(userId, dateISO) { return `qhq.briefing.${userId}.${dateISO}`; }

  static readCache(storage, userId, dateISO) {
    try {
      const raw = storage.getItem(BriefingClient.cacheKey(userId, dateISO));
      if (!raw) return null;
      return JSON.parse(raw);
    } catch (_e) { return null; }
  }

  static writeCache(storage, userId, dateISO, briefing) {
    try { storage.setItem(BriefingClient.cacheKey(userId, dateISO), JSON.stringify(briefing)); }
    catch (_e) { /* private mode / quota — cache is best-effort */ }
  }

  static guard(briefing) {
    if (!briefing || typeof briefing !== 'object') return null;
    if (typeof briefing.text !== 'string' || !briefing.text.trim()) return null;
    if (!Array.isArray(briefing.bullets)) return null;
    return briefing;
  }

  // Returns { briefing, fromCache } or { briefing: null, error }. Never throws.
  async get(userId, { force = false } = {}) {
    const day = App.utils.todayISO(0);
    const storage = window.localStorage;
    if (!force) {
      const hit = BriefingClient.guard(BriefingClient.readCache(storage, userId, day));
      if (hit) return { briefing: hit, fromCache: true };
    }
    let res;
    try { res = await this.dataStore.getBriefing(); }
    catch (err) { return { briefing: null, error: (err && err.message) || String(err) }; }
    if (!res || !res.ok) return { briefing: null, error: (res && res.error) || 'AI unavailable.' };
    const briefing = BriefingClient.guard(res.briefing);
    if (!briefing) return { briefing: null, error: 'AI returned nothing usable.' };
    BriefingClient.writeCache(storage, userId, day, briefing);
    return { briefing, fromCache: false };
  }
};
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `node --test tests/unit/briefing-cache.test.mjs`
Expected: PASS (4 tests).

- [ ] **Step 5: Add the SupabaseDataStore method**

In `js/services/SupabaseDataStore.js`, after `submitBugReport` (around line 665), add:

```javascript
  /* Daily AI briefing via the ai-assistant Edge Function. Returns
     { ok, briefing?, error? } and never throws so Home degrades gracefully. */
  async getBriefing() {
    try {
      const { data, error } = await this.supabase.functions.invoke('ai-assistant', {
        body: { action: 'briefing' },
      });
      if (error) {
        const status = (error.context && error.context.status) || null;
        let msg = (error && error.message) || 'AI unavailable.';
        try { const body = await error.context.json(); if (body && body.error) msg = body.error; }
        catch (_e) { /* body already consumed or not JSON */ }
        return { ok: false, status, error: msg };
      }
      return { ok: true, briefing: data && data.briefing };
    } catch (err) {
      return { ok: false, error: (err && err.message) || String(err) };
    }
  }
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Add the preview-mode stub**

In `js/app.js`, inside the `App.previewMode` dataStore object (after `sendEmail: async () => ({ ok: false, skipped: true }),` near line 55), add:

```javascript

```

- [ ] **Step 7: Add the script tag**

In `app.html`, immediately after the `SupabaseDataStore.js` tag (line 276), add:

```html
<script defer src="js/services/BriefingClient.js"></script>
```

- [ ] **Step 8: Re-run all unit tests**

Run: `npm run test:unit`
Expected: PASS — previous suites plus the 3 new briefing suites all green.

- [ ] **Step 9: Commit**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(ai): BriefingClient cache + dataStore.getBriefing + preview stub"
```

## Task 5: HomeView briefing card

**Files:**
- Modify: `js/views/HomeView.js`
- Modify: the Home stylesheet (the file defining `.qhq-card`/`.qhq-cc` — find with the grep below)

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `App.BriefingClient` (Task 4), `this.controller.dataStore`, `this.controller.currentUser`.
- Produces: a `.qhq-brief` card at the top of the Home main region, populated after an async fetch, with a refresh button.

- [ ] **Step 1: Locate the Home stylesheet**

Run: `grep -rl "qhq-card" css/`
Expected: one CSS file (the Home/command-center stylesheet). Note its path for Step 4.

- [ ] **Step 2: Add briefing state + render hook to HomeView**

In `js/views/HomeView.js`, in `subscribe()`'s `rerender` else-branch (around line 41), add a line to re-arm the briefing fetch on leaving Home, so it refreshes each visit:

```javascript
        this._briefFetched = false; // re-fetch the AI briefing on the next visit
```

Then, at the very start of the `qhq-cc-shell`/main region in `render()` — insert the card markup as the first child of `.qhq-cc-main` (before `qhq-col-up`). Change:

```javascript
          <div class="qhq-cc-main">
            <div class="qhq-card qhq-col-up">
```

to:

```javascript
          <div class="qhq-cc-main">
            ${this._briefingCardHtml()}
            <div class="qhq-card qhq-col-up">
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Add the card builder + lazy fetch methods**

Add these methods to the `HomeView` class (e.g. after `render()`), and a lazy-fetch block at the end of `render()`:

```javascript
  // The AI daily briefing card. Renders whatever state we currently hold:
  // skeleton while loading, the briefing once fetched, or a quiet muted line
  // if the AI is unavailable (Home never shows a broken card).
  _briefingCardHtml() {
    const esc = App.utils.escapeHtml;
    const icon = `<svg class="qhq-ic" viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true">${HOME_ICONS.fire}</svg>`;
    const head = `
      <div class="qhq-card-h">
        <span class="qhq-hicon tone-amber">${icon}</span>
        <span class="qhq-htext"><span class="ct">Daily briefing</span><span class="meta">your day at a glance</span></span>
        <button type="button" class="qhq-brief-refresh" data-brief="refresh" aria-label="Refresh briefing" title="Refresh"><i class="ti ti-refresh"></i></button>
      </div>`;

    let body;
    if (this._briefState === 'loading') {
      body = `<div class="qhq-brief-skel"><span></span><span></span><span></span></div>`;
    } else if (this._briefState === 'error' || !this._briefing) {
      body = `<div class="qhq-brief-muted">Your AI briefing isn't available right now.</div>`;
    } else {
      const b = this._briefing;
      const bullets = (b.bullets || []).map((x) =>
        `<li>${esc(x.label || '')}</li>`).join('');
      body = `
        <p class="qhq-brief-text">${esc(b.text)}</p>
        ${bullets ? `<ul class="qhq-brief-bullets">${bullets}</ul>` : ''}`;
    }
    return `<div class="qhq-card qhq-brief">${head}<div class="qhq-brief-body">${body}</div></div>`;
  }

  _fetchBriefing({ force = false } = {}) {
    if (!App.BriefingClient || !this.controller.dataStore) { this._briefState = 'error'; return; }
    this._briefState = 'loading';
    const client = this._briefClient || (this._briefClient = new App.BriefingClient({ dataStore: this.controller.dataStore }));
    client.get(this.controller.currentUser, { force }).then((r) => {
      if (r.briefing) { this._briefing = r.briefing; this._briefState = 'ready'; }
      else { this._briefState = 'error'; }
      if (this.visible()) this.render();
    });
  }
```

Then, at the end of `render()` (after the Comments lazy-load block near line 552), add the briefing lazy-load + refresh wiring:

```javascript
    // Lazy-load the AI briefing once per Home visit (cache-first inside the
    // client). Re-armed by subscribe() on leaving Home.
    if (!this._briefFetched) {
      this._briefFetched = true;
      this._fetchBriefing();
    }
    const refreshBtn = this.wrap.querySelector('[data-brief="refresh"]');
    if (refreshBtn) refreshBtn.addEventListener('click', () => this._fetchBriefing({ force: true }));
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Add card styles**

Append to the Home stylesheet located in Step 1 (reuses existing tokens — no new colors):

```css
/* AI daily briefing card */
.qhq-brief .qhq-card-h { align-items: center; }
.qhq-brief-refresh {
  margin-left: auto; background: none; border: 0; cursor: pointer;
  color: var(--ink-3, #7a8390); padding: 4px; border-radius: 8px; line-height: 0;
}
.qhq-brief-refresh:hover { color: var(--amber, #ED4E0D); background: var(--bg-3, #f2f4f7); }
.qhq-brief-body { padding: 4px 2px 2px; }
.qhq-brief-text { margin: 0; line-height: 1.55; color: var(--ink-1, #1c2430); }
.qhq-brief-bullets { margin: 10px 0 0; padding-left: 18px; display: grid; gap: 4px; }
.qhq-brief-bullets li { line-height: 1.4; color: var(--ink-2, #46505d); }
.qhq-brief-muted { color: var(--ink-3, #7a8390); font-size: 0.92em; }
.qhq-brief-skel { display: grid; gap: 8px; }
.qhq-brief-skel span { height: 12px; border-radius: 6px; background: var(--bg-3, #f2f4f7); }
.qhq-brief-skel span:nth-child(1) { width: 92%; }
.qhq-brief-skel span:nth-child(2) { width: 84%; }
.qhq-brief-skel span:nth-child(3) { width: 60%; }
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) {
  .qhq-brief-skel span { animation: qhq-brief-pulse 1.2s ease-in-out infinite; }
}
@keyframes qhq-brief-pulse { 0%,100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: 0.55; } }
```

Confirm the token fallbacks (`--ink-1`, `--bg-3`, `--amber`) match the ones used elsewhere in that stylesheet; if the file uses different token names, use those instead (the `#hex` fallbacks keep it correct regardless).

- [ ] **Step 5: Manual verification (preview mode)**

Run the dev server: `npm run dev`, open the app in preview mode, land on Home.
Expected: the briefing card renders its header + refresh button, briefly shows the skeleton, then falls to the muted "isn't available right now" line (preview stub returns unavailable). No console errors, no layout overflow at ≤720px. Toggle `prefers-reduced-motion` and confirm the skeleton stops pulsing.

```bash

git commit -m "feat(ai): Home daily-briefing card (skeleton + refresh + graceful degrade)"
```

## Task 6: Deploy the function + live smoke test

**Files:** none (deploy + verification).

- [ ] **Step 1: Set secrets + deploy to PROD**

```bash
# Ensure the Supabase CLI is linked to PROD qqvmcsvdxhgjooirznrj first.
supabase secrets set GROQ_API_KEY=gsk_xxx
supabase secrets set ALLOWED_ORIGINS="https://<prod-origin>,http://localhost:5173"
supabase functions deploy ai-assistant
```

Expected: deploy succeeds; `supabase functions list` shows `ai-assistant`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Curl smoke test — no JWT is rejected**

```bash
curl -i -X POST "https://qqvmcsvdxhgjooirznrj.supabase.co/functions/v1/ai-assistant" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "apikey: <anon-key>" \
  -d '{"action":"briefing"}'
```

Expected: `401` `{"error":"Not signed in."}` (or `401 Missing authorization.`), NOT a briefing.

- [ ] **Step 3: Curl smoke test — signed-in user gets a briefing**

Obtain a real user access token (sign in via the app, copy the `access_token` from the Supabase session in devtools/localStorage), then:

```bash
curl -i -X POST "https://qqvmcsvdxhgjooirznrj.supabase.co/functions/v1/ai-assistant" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "apikey: <anon-key>" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <user-access-token>" \
  -d '{"action":"briefing"}'
```

Expected: `200` with `{ ok: true, briefing: { text, bullets, source }, generatedAt }`. `source` is `"model"` when Groq responds, `"fallback"` if the key/provider is misconfigured (still a valid card — investigate the logs if so).

- [ ] **Step 4: Unknown action is rejected**

```bash
curl -i -X POST "https://qqvmcsvdxhgjooirznrj.supabase.co/functions/v1/ai-assistant" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "apikey: <anon-key>" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <user-access-token>" \
  -d '{"action":"nope"}'
```

Expected: `400 {"error":"Unknown action."}`.

- [ ] **Step 5: Merge client + live QA**

Merge the client changes to `main` (Vercel auto-deploys). On the live URL, sign in and land on Home.
Expected: the briefing card shows the skeleton, then a real 2–4 sentence briefing + up to 3 bullets referencing the signed-in user's actual tasks. Verify desktop + mobile (≤720px), light + dark, and reduced-motion. Reload — the card renders instantly from cache (no second network call the same day); the refresh button forces a regenerate.

- [ ] **Step 6: Final commit / done**

No code change here; record completion in the AI assistant program memory and note the deploy verification result.

---

## Notes / rollback

- The whole feature fails safe: if `ai-assistant` is undeployed, the key is missing, or Groq is down, `getBriefing` returns `{ ok: false }`, the client guard yields null, and Home shows the muted line — nothing breaks. Rolling back is removing the script tag + card, or simply leaving the function undeployed.
- Privacy: the viewer's own task **titles + metadata** are sent to Groq (US-hosted). Accepted trade-off for the free tier (recorded in the spec).
